TEXT.id: PaiwanCh2_049_ORIGIN OF THE FIVE YEAR FESTIVAL
TEXT.citation: Early, R. J., & Whitehorn, J. (2003). One hundred Paiwan texts. Pacific Linguistics, Research School of Pacific and Asian Studies, The Australian National University.
TEXT.BibTeX_citation: @book{100paiwantexts, author = {Robert Early and John Whitehorn}, title = {One Hundred Paiwan Texts}, year = {2003}, publisher = {Pacific Linguistics, Research School of Pacific and Asian Studies, The Australian National University}}

S.FORM: naizua ti sa Rungiɫingan a natsemukatsukar.
naizua ti sa Rungiljingan a nacemukacukar.
S.TRANSL: There was (in heaven (OA)) a person called Rungiɫingan who had been drying some clothes.

S.FORM: matjani a ɫeteku a sema Lingutjaɫ.
matjani a ljeteku a sema Lingutjalj.
S.TRANSL: He fell down to the Lingutjaɫ house,

S.FORM: sa djumaken ni sa Łemud.
sa djumaken ni sa Łemud.
S.TRANSL: and was found by Łemud.

S.FORM: "kemasinu sun?"
"kemasinu sun?"
S.TRANSL: “Where are you from?”

S.FORM: "kemasi tjarivavaw aken.
"kemasi tjarivavaw aken.
S.TRANSL: “I’m from heaven.

S.FORM: pai petsunguan aken, uɫa ken a kiayan tua tsevuɫ a sema tjarivavaw."
pai pecunguan aken, ulja ken a kiayan tua cevulj a sema tjarivavaw."
S.TRANSL: Light a fire for me, so that I can go up to heaven with the smoke.”

S.FORM: sa petsungu anga.
sa pecungu anga.
S.TRANSL: He lit a fire.

S.FORM: "nu sika lima sa kiayan anga tua tsevuɫ, uri seman pulingaw amen.
"nu sika lima sa kiayan anga tua cevulj, uri seman pulingaw amen.
S.TRANSL: “On the fifth day, come up with the smoke; we will make (you) a shaman.

S.FORM: nu sika lima sa nu mangtjez sun i tjarivavaw, tjukatsasavanu anga; sa kilangdangda-i anga nu malada men.
nu sika lima sa nu mangtjez sun i tjarivavaw, tjukacasavanu anga; sa kilangdangda-i anga nu malada men.
S.TRANSL: On the fifth day when you come to heaven, stay in the courtyard, and listen while we perform the rite.

S.FORM: nu talangtangtang, sa tjiɫivaru anga, nu uri nemingats anga men."
nu talangtangtang, sa tjiljivaru anga, nu uri nemingac anga men."
S.TRANSL: When it’s getting light, go in the indoor granary, when we are beginning to recite(?).

S.FORM: saka i tjiɫivar anga ti sa Łemud, a patsutsun tua kisan pulingalingaw.
saka i tjiljivar anga ti sa Łemud, a pacucun tua kisan pulingalingaw.
S.TRANSL: So Łemud was in the granary watching the rite of making a shaman.

S.FORM: ka maɫia, "ki ken a vaik anan," aya ti sa Łemud.
ka maljia, "ki ken a vaik anan," aya ti sa Łemud.
S.TRANSL: Next day Łemud said: “I’ll be off now.”

S.FORM: "anema a nu vusam, anema nu tjaɫayan?"
"anema a nu vusam, anema nu tjaljayan?"
S.TRANSL: “What small and bigger seeds do you have?”

S.FORM: "qaliḍuḍu, vala."
"qalidrudru, vala."
S.TRANSL: “We have qaliḍuḍu and vala taro.”

S.FORM: "katsu tusa vusam, a tjelu a vusaman, a vuluvulung, a tjamekuɫ, a ɫailiman.
"kacu tusa vusam, a tjelu a vusaman, a vuluvulung, a tjamekulj, a ljailiman.
S.TRANSL: “Take these small seeds of three kinds: vuluvulung, tjamekuɫ and ɫailiman millet.

S.FORM: ari sa-u tjua Qutsuɫi.
ari sa-u tjua Quculji.
S.TRANSL: Off you go to Qutsuɫi.

S.FORM: katsu tua ḍiḍi a vulivuliqan."
kacu tua dridri a vulivuliqan."
S.TRANSL: Take a dappled pig.”

S.FORM: "maɫevɫeveq amen," ayain ni sa Rungiɫingan.
"maljevljeveq amen," ayain ni sa Rungiljingan.
S.TRANSL: “We are celebrating the five year festival,” added Rungiɫingan;

S.FORM: "alau pai kaɫevequ anga, sa tsektseku anga tua kasiw."
"alau pai kaljevequ anga, sa cekceku anga tua kasiw."
S.TRANSL: “so you too celebrate it, and stick wooden stakes in the ground.”

S.FORM: sa vaik anga ti sa Łemud a sema tjariteku.
sa vaik anga ti sa Łemud a sema tjariteku.
S.TRANSL: Łemud went back to the world below.

S.FORM: mangtjez ti sa Łemud.
mangtjez ti sa Łemud.
S.TRANSL: He arrived;

S.FORM: tsemektsek tua kasiw; mangtjez i Livulivuan.
cemekcek tua kasiw; mangtjez i Livulivuan.
S.TRANSL: he stuck stakes in. He got to Livulivuan,

S.FORM: sa vaik a sema Rusivavan uta tsemektsek.
sa vaik a sema Rusivavan uta cemekcek.
S.TRANSL: and went to Rusivavan sticking in stakes.

S.FORM: saka "nakuya tsu a neka nu pulingaw," aya.
saka "nakuya cu a neka nu pulingaw," aya.
S.TRANSL: “It’s bad there are no shamans,” he (or they) said;

S.FORM: sa kisan pulingaw a marekakakaka a maɫe lima, a siruvetjek ni sa Łemud.
sa kisan pulingaw a marekakakaka a malje lima, a siruvetjek ni sa Łemud.
S.TRANSL: and five siblings, Łemud’s siblings, became shamans.

S.FORM: sa tulu-i tuazua kinitulu a kasi tjarivavaw; sa patagiɫ anga uta maɫeveq a i tjariteku.
sa tulu-i tuazua kinitulu a kasi tjarivavaw; sa patagilj anga uta maljeveq a i tjariteku.
S.TRANSL: And he taught them what he had learnt from heaven; and they began to celebrate the festival in the world below too.

S.FORM: saka izua.
saka izua.
S.TRANSL: And it goes on.

S.FORM: saka amin.
saka amin.
S.TRANSL: The end.
